response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial data experience (founder of an open-source Geo-Data platform used by Green Map, GIS web applications, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and fibers. However, that systems work was in DLang rather than modern C++ or Rust, his commercial C++ exposure is limited and dated (National Instruments, 2014-2015), and his recent roles are TypeScript/Node full-stack product work. There is no direct routing or navigation guidance experience, so fit hinges on how transferable his D-language systems and geo-data skills prove in a technical interview.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G talk)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, caching)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source tooling, Linux fluency
concerns: Modern commercial C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust
No direct routing, map-tiling, or navigation guidance background
Recent focus (2020+) is TypeScript/Node full-stack, away from systems programming
